claim: Progress from Prompt to Prompt Chain to AI Agent on a spectrum; start simple and only add complexity (prompt chains, then agents) when simpler solutions fail.
actionable: For your problem, implement a Prompt first; measure success; only move to Prompt Chain if results are unsatisfactory; only build an AI Agent if chains fail benchmarks.
source_title: AGE OF AGENTS: How to EVOLVE your o3-mini Prompts into Multi-Tool AI Agents

claim: Breaking large problems (e.g., 350K-token transcript) into smaller slices enables parallel execution and makes problems tractable for LLMs.
actionable: Chunk large inputs into 'slices' (sentence-level pieces); send each slice to an agent/prompt in parallel; merge results back together to avoid token/context limits.
source_title: AGE OF AGENTS: How to EVOLVE your o3-mini Prompts into Multi-Tool AI Agents

claim: Prompt chains require tracking state (original content, current edits, edits made so far) and iterating until an exit condition is met (e.g., 'no deletions needed').
actionable: In a prompt chain loop, pass: original slice, current_deletions (list of changes made), iteration_slice (result after deletions). Update state each loop iteration.
source_title: AGE OF AGENTS: How to EVOLVE your o3-mini Prompts into Multi-Tool AI Agents

claim: O3-mini achieves 80% accuracy on video-editing problems with a simple Prompt; GPT-4o struggles at 20%; but cost/quality tradeoff and thinking tokens matter.
actionable: Test reasoning models (o3-mini) vs base models (GPT-4o) on your specific evals; reasoning models often outperform despite higher token costs when thinking is enabled.
source_title: AGE OF AGENTS: How to EVOLVE your o3-mini Prompts into Multi-Tool AI Agents

claim: More compute (prompt chains, agents) doesn't always improve results; some problems solved correctly by a simple Prompt fail when run as an Agent.
actionable: Benchmark your problem across all three levels (Prompt, Chain, Agent) with consistent evals; don't assume the complex approach is better.
source_title: AGE OF AGENTS: How to EVOLVE your o3-mini Prompts into Multi-Tool AI Agents

claim: AI Agents need limited compute loops (e.g., max 16 iterations) with explicit exit tools (complete_edit, reset_to_original) to prevent runaway loops.
actionable: Set a max_iterations parameter; give agents explicit tools to signal 'done' or 'reset'; log all tool calls to detect loops or misuse.
source_title: AGE OF AGENTS: How to EVOLVE your o3-mini Prompts into Multi-Tool AI Agents

claim: Over-editing in prompt chains (e.g., deleting too much text) happens when chains keep looping without proper stopping conditions.
actionable: Include explicit stopping logic in prompt chain instructions: 'If no more edits are needed, return an empty list and exit.' Monitor logs for runaway behavior.
source_title: AGE OF AGENTS: How to EVOLVE your o3-mini Prompts into Multi-Tool AI Agents

claim: Including examples in prompts guides subjective/creative decisions better than generic instructions, improving consistency with desired style/taste.
actionable: Add an <examples> section to prompts showing correct edits/outputs; include 3-5 annotated examples of the style you want the model to learn.
source_title: AGE OF AGENTS: How to EVOLVE your o3-mini Prompts into Multi-Tool AI Agents

claim: Training agents to know when NOT to act (e.g., 'no edits needed, leave it unchanged') is as important as training them to act.
actionable: Explicitly mention in prompts/system instructions: 'Sometimes the best action is no action. If the input is already correct, do nothing.' Test with examples.
source_title: AGE OF AGENTS: How to EVOLVE your o3-mini Prompts into Multi-Tool AI Agents

claim: Structured outputs (Zod, JSON schema) ensure consistent response formats across prompts, chains, and agents, enabling scaling and chaining.
actionable: Define a Zod schema or JSON schema for all outputs; use structured output tools in your LLM API calls; ensure every level (prompt/chain/agent) returns the same type.
source_title: AGE OF AGENTS: How to EVOLVE your o3-mini Prompts into Multi-Tool AI Agents

claim: Benchmarking (evals) across prompt/chain/agent is critical; without evals, you won't know which approach is actually better for your problem.
actionable: Build a small test suite (10+ representative problems); run your prompt, chain, and agent against each; track success rate, cost, and latency.
source_title: AGE OF AGENTS: How to EVOLVE your o3-mini Prompts into Multi-Tool AI Agents

claim: Anthropic's 'Building Effective Agents' guide recommends: start with prompts, add workflows/chains when prompts fail, only then consider full agents.
actionable: Reference Anthropic's guide; follow the hierarchy strictly; document why you moved from one level to the next based on eval results.
source_title: AGE OF AGENTS: How to EVOLVE your o3-mini Prompts into Multi-Tool AI Agents
